dimensions.name: testability
dimensions.evidence: Most acceptance criteria are mechanically checkable with explicit exit codes, file paths, JSON fields, and command outputs.

dimensions.name: completeness
dimensions.evidence: All three deliverables in `.furrow/rows/pi-row-audit-cleanup/definition.yaml` have dedicated spec sections with interface contracts and refined acceptance criteria.

dimensions.name: consistency
dimensions.evidence: The specs conflict on core interfaces: `repair-deliverables-cli` requires `deliverables[].evidence_path` and conflict-exit behavior, while `pi-step-ceremony-backfill` emits `evidence_paths` lists and `repair-deliverables-cli` later defines skip-on-conflict partial-repair semantics.

dimensions.name: test-scenario-coverage
dimensions.evidence: Several non-trivial archive ACs lack explicit WHEN/THEN scenarios with verification commands, notably `.furrow/.focused` clearing and the no-`supersedes` regression guard.

dimensions.name: implementability
dimensions.evidence: A developer still has to ask which manifest schema to implement and where the audit trail lives, because the spec alternates between `state.json.repair_audit` and a `repair-audit.jsonl` sidecar despite `schemas/state.schema.json` forbidding new top-level fields.
